# UC-22.7.19 · Business Process Impact — Incidents by Critical Application

## Description

Aggregates security incidents by business-critical application to inform risk assessment prioritization under Identify.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST CSF compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Populate dest-to-app mapping; (2) refresh after architecture changes; (3) present in risk workshop; (4) tie to BIA refresh cycle. Lookups: `app_criticality_lookup`, `app_criticality_lookup.csv`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `notable`; `app_criticality_lookup.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Populate dest-to-app mapping; (2) refresh after architecture changes; (3) present in risk workshop; (4) tie to BIA refresh cycle.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
search `notable` earliest=-90d
| lookup app_criticality_lookup.csv dest OUTPUT criticality
| stats count by criticality, dest
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Business Process Impact — Incidents by Critical Application (ID.RA-01)** — Aggregates security incidents by business-critical application to inform risk assessment prioritization under Identify.

Documented **Data sources**: `notable`; `app_criticality_lookup.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by criticality, dest** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with ES Incident Review for the relevant correlation. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart, Table

## SPL

```spl
search `notable` earliest=-90d
| lookup app_criticality_lookup.csv dest OUTPUT criticality
| stats count by criticality, dest
| sort - count
```

Bar chart, Table. Key fields: `criticality`, `dest`.
